text:
The Citadel, originally known as The Citadel of Ricks, was a trans-dimensional citadel city-state inhabited entirely by Ricks and Mortys from across infinite realities. The Citadel worked like Furp Rock; located in a pocket dimension not related to any reality. You can reach the Citadel from any dimension of the Central Finite Curve reaching the certain coordinates in space.

The Citadel was formed as a secret entity serving to protect Ricks and Mortys from a multitude of accumulated enemies within the Galaxy, including Rick C-137 and the Galactic Federation. Prior to the election of President Morty The Citadel was controlled by the Council of Ricks and, after the partial destruction of The Citadel during a conflict with the Federation, the secretive Shadow Council.

The Citadel was populated by Ricks and Mortys from across the Central Finite Curve and utilised as a leisure place, meeting point and place of protection for many Ricks and Mortys. However, the Citadel also served as a permanent residence for an untold number of Ricks and Mortys that decided to settle at there. Though its true population remained unknown and has never been mentioned, Rick once stated "Whoever is still exploring the Citadel, is either stupid, or one of the unfortunate millions held hostage by their terrible ideas," suggesting that the population count was well into the millions (although he was probably referring to the audience).

Mortys were revealed to be mass produced as clones in the Citadel to be dispatched to Ricks as sidekicks and cloaking devices to hide Ricks from dangers. The clones were needed for those Ricks whose original Mortys died or came from universes where Morty was never born. These Mortys were imbued with desirable subservient traits described as being 'bred for forgiveness'. The Citadel also influenced Beth and Jerry's relationship in various universes to make sure there were still a steady supply of naturally-born Mortys.

In "The Rickshank Rickdemption" Rick C-137 teleported the Citadel in Galactic Federation Prison in his dimension which destroyed both locations causing chaos and conflict between two fractions. By the event's of "Ricklantis Mixup" the Citadel had been rebuilt. Evil Morty, hiding his true identity and motives, ran for president under the "Morty Party" under the guise of unity and populism. Following an assassination attempt by his former campaign manager who learned his identity, Evil Morty won the election by a close margin and became the president of the Citadel.

Following the events of "The Rickshank Rickdemption", The Citadel came under the authoritarian rule of Evil Morty who, after being elected president of the Citadel, committed a series of unprecedented purges against the de facto ruling Shadow Council and other personal political enemies. From this point up until its destruction, President Morty had remained the absolute dictatorial leader of The Citadel.

In "Rickmurai Jack" the Citadel was ultimately destroyed after Evil Morty set off a chain reaction of events that kills all the Ricks and most of the Mortys in the Citadel and ultimately exploded after Evil Morty broke through the Central Finite Curve. Rick C-137 and Morty managed to detach Mortyburg from the rest of the city and was ultimately spared destruction however.

Following the Citadel's destruction, the remains became a refugee shelter for cloned Ricks and Mortys created in the Citadel who were teleported there after the portal reset in "Solaricks" because they didn't have a home reality to return to. In "The Rick, The Mort & The Ugly" one of the Rick clones, Boss Hog Rick, attempted to rebuild a new Citadel out of the ruins of the old one. This all came crashing down, however, when Homesteader Rick destroyed the under-construction Citadel and killed Boss Hog Rick and all of his workers in order to give his enslaved cloned Mortys a chance at life without Ricks.

Through various universes, Rick has been involved through resistance groups against the Galactic Federation, as well as other groups who consider his genius a threat, such as galactic terrorists, sub-galactic dictators and most of the entire Intergalactic government. To protect themselves and hide from opposing groups, a "few thousand" alternate versions of Rick decided to join together and create their own society. In its early stages, this society came into conflict with Rick C-137 who began killing many of its members as he hunted for Rick Prime, the Rick he believed to have killed his family. After C-137 had killed a great many of his counterparts, he and the alliance brokered a peace treaty, one that saw C-137 help design and construct the Citadel. The Citadel is known to have existed for at least 15 years, which predates main Morty's birth. As revealed in Season 5, different dimensions have Ricks and Mortys at varying ages so Mortys may have existed when it was founded.
